D3 Chemistry L4 Analyze Reference

Hiding Power — Pigment/Vehicle Refractive Index Mismatch

white paint hides because TiO₂ n≈2.7 scatters strongly against vehicle n≈1.5; the bigger the index mismatch (Δn), the more opaque the dry film

A pigment particle scatters light when its refractive index differs from the surrounding vehicle (resin + solvent). Scattering cross-section ~ (n_p − n_v)². A high-Δn pigment like TiO₂ (n≈2.7) in alkyd or acrylic (n≈1.5) gives extreme scattering — that's why titanium-white hides like nothing else. Match indices and the pigment goes invisible: glass beads in water, sugar in water.

Drag the pigment refractive index. Δn = n_p − n_v drives the scattering bar and the simulated film opacity. Markers: TiO₂ 2.70, ZnO 2.0, CaCO₃ 1.6, glass-bead 1.55 (in 1.50 vehicle).
